# Spec — 2026-05-18

## Executive Summary

- **All 5 items from 2026-05-16 are now resolved** in our codebase: `continueOnBlock` hook feedback, chunked compaction, stop hook block cap, hook event-type validation, and `{file:...}` agent prompt syntax
- **anthropics/claude-code** (v2.1.139-143): No new release since v2.1.143 (May 15). Key patterns from v2.1.139-141 still worth adopting: `/goal` command (autonomous loop with completion condition), rewind/partial-summarize menu, compaction prompt preserving sensitive user instructions, `terminalSequence` hook field for desktop notifications, MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT properly raising per-request timeout for remote HTTP/SSE servers
- **cline/cline** (v3.0.3→v3.0.7, May 14-18): `--worktree` CLI flag for auto-creating git worktrees per task; ChatGPT OAuth model catalog; plugin tools/commands hydration in settings; light theme TUI color improvements
- **Kilo-Org/kilocode** (May 16-18): Only 3 commits — NVIDIA NIM origin header support; docs/beta messaging removal. No significant new patterns
- **aider-ai/aider** (May 15-16): Bash tree-sitter tags for repomap support (single commit, already noted in previous research)
- **continuedev/continue**: No new commits since April 17. Project appears largely dormant
- **Key new gap**: MCP tool call timeout — our `McpClientManager.callTool()` has no configurable timeout; a slow or hung MCP server blocks the session indefinitely. Claude Code fixed this in v2.1.142
- **Key new gap**: `--worktree` CLI flag — both Cline and Claude Code now support auto-creating a git worktree for each task; our CLI has no equivalent. This enables safe parallel agent runs without file conflicts
- **Key new gap**: Compaction instruction preservation — Claude Code's compaction prompt now explicitly asks the model to preserve sensitive user instructions (API keys, preferences, constraints). Our `SUMMARY_PROMPT` omits this
- **Key new pattern**: Goal-driven autonomous loop (`/goal`) — set a completion condition and the agent iterates across turns until met, with live overlay showing elapsed/turns/tokens. Our CLI has no autonomous loop command

## What to build (prioritized)

### 1. MCP Tool Call Timeout
- **What**: Add a configurable timeout to `McpClientManager.callTool()` that aborts MCP tool calls exceeding the limit. Default 60s, configurable via `MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT` env var or per-server config. Also apply to the initial `connect()` handshake
- **Why**: Our `callTool()` at `src/mcp/client.ts:391` calls `connection.client.callTool()` with no timeout — a hung MCP server (network failure, infinite computation, unresponsive remote) blocks the entire agent turn with no recovery path. Claude Code v2.1.142 fixed exactly this: "Fixed `MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT` not raising the per-request fetch timeout for remote HTTP and SSE MCP servers, which capped tool calls at 60 seconds regardless of the configured value"
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.142 MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT fix
- **Category**: mcp
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

### 2. CLI --worktree Flag for Task Isolation
- **What**: Add a `--worktree` flag to the CLI that auto-creates a detached-HEAD git worktree before running the task, enabling parallel agent sessions without file conflicts. Clean up worktree on completion
- **Why**: Both Claude Code (`worktree.bgIsolation`) and Cline (`--worktree` flag, v3.0.3+) support worktree-based isolation. Cline's implementation (`src/utils/git-worktree.ts`) creates worktrees at `~/.cline/worktrees/<uuid>/<repoName>/`. Our CLI has no `--worktree` flag and our agent-manager references `worktreeId` but doesn't auto-create them from the CLI. This is a prerequisite for safe parallel autonomous runs
- **Source**: cline/cline commit 59e5eac3 "feat(cli): add --worktree flag to auto-create and run in a new git worktree"
- **Category**: command
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days)

### 3. Compaction Instruction Preservation Prompt
- **What**: Enhance the `SUMMARY_PROMPT` in `src/compaction/index.ts` to explicitly instruct the model to preserve sensitive user instructions, preferences, constraints, and configuration details verbatim in the summary
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.139 added "Compaction prompt now asks the model to preserve sensitive user instructions". Our current `SUMMARY_PROMPT` at `src/compaction/index.ts:105` focuses on summarizing conversation history but doesn't explicitly tell the model to preserve user-specified constraints (e.g., "always use tabs", "never modify X file", API endpoints). After compaction, these instructions vanish and the model violates user preferences. One-line prompt change with high impact
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.139 "Compaction prompt now asks the model to preserve sensitive user instructions"
- **Category**: core
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

### 4. Goal-Driven Autonomous Loop Command
- **What**: Add a `/goal` command that accepts a completion condition (natural language or regex predicate), then iterates autonomously across turns until the condition is met. Show a live overlay with elapsed time, turn count, and token usage. Allow cancellation with Ctrl+C/Esc
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.139 shipped `/goal` — "set a completion condition and Claude keeps working across turns until it's met. Works in interactive, `-p`, and Remote Control". Our orchestrator handles single turns and expects the user to manually re-prompt. For batch tasks (fix all lint errors, migrate all files, run tests until green), the user must babysit each iteration. A `/goal` command automates multi-turn convergence toward a defined condition
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.139 `/goal` command
- **Category**: command
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days)

### 5. Rewind / Partial Summarize ("Summarize up to here")
- **What**: Add a rewind menu to the TUI that lets the user select a turn boundary and either (a) discard all turns after that point, or (b) summarize all turns before that point while keeping recent turns intact
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.141 added "Rewind menu: added 'Summarize up to here' to compress earlier context while keeping recent turns intact". Our TUI has no rewind capability — once context grows large, the user's only option is full compaction (which summarizes everything) or starting a new session. Partial summarize gives fine-grained control over what the model remembers
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.141 "Rewind menu: added 'Summarize up to here'"
- **Category**: command
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days)
